theory.canonical_statement: ID5760 records Holonomy Lagrangian Evolution Equation as a source-extracted canonical equation.
theory.technical_interpretation: The entry preserves the equation exactly as extracted from `00_Engine\Lemma-Maker\Prism\topological_quantum_storage_paper.tex` for registry alignment and later derivation.

latex_blocks: $: \begin{equation} \mathcal{L}(C_1,C_2) = \frac{1}{4\pi}\oint_{C_1}\oint_{C_2} \frac{d\mathbf{r}_1 \times d\mathbf{r}_2 \cdot (\mathbf{r}_1-\mathbf{r}_2)}{|\mathbf{r}_1-\mathbf{r}_2|^3} \in \mathbb{Z} \end{equation} This topological invariant is quantized and robust against continuous deformations. \section{Frame-Dragging Analog} At extreme rotation rates $
canonical_equation.source_section_latex: $: \begin{equation} \mathcal{L}(C_1,C_2) = \frac{1}{4\pi}\oint_{C_1}\oint_{C_2} \frac{d\mathbf{r}_1 \times d\mathbf{r}_2 \cdot (\mathbf{r}_1-\mathbf{r}_2)}{|\mathbf{r}_1-\mathbf{r}_2|^3} \in \mathbb{Z} \end{equation} This topological invariant is quantized and robust against continuous deformations. \section{Frame-Dragging Analog} At extreme rotation rates $

wolfram_form.existing_from_tex: $: \begin{equation} \mathcal{L}(C_1,C_2) = \frac{1}{4\pi}\oint_{C_1}\oint_{C_2} \frac{d\mathbf{r}_1 \times d\mathbf{r}_2 \cdot (\mathbf{r}_1-\mathbf{r}_2)}{|\mathbf{r}_1-\mathbf{r}_2|^3} \in \mathbb{Z} \end{equation} This topological invariant is quantized and robust against continuous deformations. \section{Frame-Dragging Analog} At extreme rotation rates $
